technique: Privilege escalation - account/host gaining rights it should not have (admin-group add, special-privilege grant, GPO/AdminSDHolder abuse, computer-account exploitation, AD CS)

trigger.from_breadth: A privileged-group membership add (4728/4732/4756) to Domain Admins / Enterprise Admins / Administrators; OR 4672 special-privilege assignment for an account that does not normally hold it; OR 5136/4739 GPO / domain-policy / AdminSDHolder modification; OR computer-account anomalies (4742 DC machine-account password reset = Zerologon-style; 4741 machine-account create + 4781 rename = noPac); OR 4886/4887 AD CS certificate anomaly. Also chains from a confirmed compromise or recon -> did the foothold escalate?

telemetry_required: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id 4728/4732/4756 (group add), 4672/4673 (special privileges), 5136/4739 (DS object / domain policy modified), 4742/4741/4781 (computer account), 4886/4887 (AD CS cert request/issue)
winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security + event_data SubjectUserName / TargetUserName / MemberName / GroupName
telemetry_gap_behavior: KNOWN-GAP, never clean: Kerberos 4768/4769 are DROPPED at collection -> AD CS cert-based auth escalation and Kerberos-driven escalation are PARTLY BLIND (detect the cert request/issue 4886/4887 and the group/privilege change, but not the ticket use). No Sysmon -> token manipulation (T1134), UAC bypass (T1548), and process injection (T1055) are BLIND -> lean on ESET LSASS/injection signal + 4672/4673, and RECOMMEND enabling Sysmon + DS-object-change auditing. If 5136 is not collected on a GL, note the AdminSDHolder/GPO-audit gap.

method.compute: the added MemberName, the GroupName (is it Domain Admins / Enterprise Admins / Schema Admins / Administrators / Account Operators?), and the SubjectUserName who added it; was the added account previously non-privileged?
method.decision_input: a non-privileged account added to a domain/forest admin group = direct privilege escalation -> CRITICAL

method.compute: does this account normally receive special (admin-equivalent) privileges at logon? first-ever 4672 for a standard user = newly-elevated
method.decision_input: first-seen special-privilege assignment for an account that never had it = escalation signal (correlate with how it was granted)

method.compute: which object was modified - a GPO (groupPolicyContainer), the AdminSDHolder container, or a protected/privileged object DACL? who modified it?
method.decision_input: GPO or AdminSDHolder modification by a non-GPO-admin / non-tier0 account = stealth escalation / persistence -> CRITICAL

method.compute: 4742 resetting a DOMAIN CONTROLLER machine-account password (esp. from a non-DC / anonymous source) = Zerologon-style; 4741 machine-account creation by a standard user + 4781 rename = noPac (CVE-2021-42278/42287)
method.decision_input: DC machine-account password reset by a non-DC, or machine-account create+rename by a normal user, = CVE exploitation -> CRITICAL

method.goal: AD CS certificate abuse (ESC*) - partial (Kerberos auth blind)
method.compute: a certificate request/issue where the requester != the subject identity, or a privileged/any-purpose template used by a standard user (ESC1)
method.decision_input: cert issued for another identity / privileged template to a normal user = AD CS escalation. NOTE: the subsequent cert-based logon (4768 w/ cert) is DROPPED -> flag the request/issue + KNOWN-GAP on the auth

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: non-privileged account added to a domain/forest admin group; AdminSDHolder/GPO modified by a non-tier0 account; DC machine-account password reset (Zerologon); noPac machine-account create+rename; AD CS cert issued to a normal user for another identity
decision.HIGH_conf4: first-seen 4672 admin privileges for an account that never had them, correlated with a prior compromise/recon; GPO modification by a non-GPO-admin
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: an isolated privileged-group change or 4672 that may be legitimate admin/PIM activity - review against the change record
decision.FP_kill: approved admin group changes (ticketed); PIM/JIT just-in-time elevation; GPO edits by designated GPO admins in a change window; AAD Connect / backup machine-account operations; AD CS auto-enrollment of standard machine/user certs

false_positive_checks: approved/ticketed admin-group membership changes and PIM/JIT just-in-time role activation
designated GPO administrators editing GPOs during a change window
AAD Connect sync (MSOL_*/AAD_*) and backup service machine-account operations
AD CS auto-enrollment issuing routine machine/user certificates (template + requester==subject)
tier-0 admin accounts that legitimately hold special privileges (baseline the account)

output_findings_json.query_template: winlogbeat_winlog_event_id:(4728 OR 4732 OR 4756 OR 5136 OR 4742 OR 4741) AND winlogbeat_winlog_channel:Security
